siteProfile.name: Your Name
siteProfile.headline: Hi, I'm Your Name
siteProfile.positioning: A file-driven portfolio template for publishing project cards, case studies, and technical notes without rebuilding the app.
siteProfile.intro: This template is designed for developers who want a portfolio that behaves like a lightweight content system. Projects and blog posts live in local files, can be mounted into Docker at runtime, and stay connected through tags and related project metadata.
workingStyle: Keep content close to the codebase, but load it at runtime.
Separate card metadata from long-form markdown detail pages.
Use related tags to connect project evidence with technical articles.

timelineItems.title: Foundation
timelineItems.summary: Replace this item with your education, self-learning, or first project milestone.

timelineItems.title: First Systems
timelineItems.summary: Describe how your early experiments became usable tools or project evidence.

timelineItems.title: Delivery Practice
timelineItems.summary: Summarize real workflow, product, or team experience that shaped your engineering style.

timelineItems.title: Case Studies
timelineItems.summary: Turn completed projects into readable case studies with outcomes and tradeoffs.

timelineItems.title: Public Portfolio
timelineItems.summary: Publish project pages, blog notes, screenshots, and benchmarks as reusable proof.

skillItems.name: Frontend Systems
skillItems.note: Replace this with evidence from your UI, app shell, and interaction work.

skillItems.name: Content Workflow
skillItems.note: Projects and posts are editable through files and can be mounted at runtime.

skillItems.name: Backend/API
skillItems.note: Use this row for API, server actions, database, or integration experience.

skillItems.name: Performance
skillItems.note: Document benchmark evidence, bundle size, image strategy, and caching choices.

skillItems.name: Security
skillItems.note: Raw HTML is disabled in markdown and asset paths are constrained to content folders.

skillItems.name: Documentation
skillItems.note: README, project detail pages, and blog posts explain design decisions.

resumeSummary: A sanitized resume template for presenting experience, skills, project highlights, and contact information.

resumeExperience.title: Software Engineer / Portfolio Owner
resumeExperience.description: Replace this with your real experience summary. Focus on ownership, scope, systems delivered, and measurable results.
highlights: Built and maintained project pages through file-based metadata and markdown content.
Connected project case studies with blog articles through tags and related project fields.
Validated the template with typecheck, lint, build, and dependency audit checks.

resumeExperience.title: Project Contributor
resumeExperience.organization: Team, School, Client, or Open Source
resumeExperience.description: Use this entry for a second experience block. Keep it outcome-oriented and specific.
highlights: Implemented a feature from requirements to release.
Improved documentation, testing, or deployment workflow.
Captured tradeoffs and future work in a project detail page.

adjustmentNotes.text: Projects and blog posts are loaded from files at runtime.
Markdown raw HTML is disabled for safer template usage.
Content folders can be mounted into Docker without rebuilding.

hero.description: A file-driven portfolio template for publishing project cards, case studies, and technical notes without rebuilding the app.
